title: Chapter 3: Shadow among Ashes
paragraphs: The ash crunched nauseatingly between the teeth. The cold, bone-chilling wind howled over the wastelands, lifting gray, stifling swirls of radioactive dust into the air. Avotu had been walking westward for two days now, mechanically putting one foot in front of the other in his heavy plate armor. As a fire elf, whose physiology was far superior to that of a human, he required neither sleep nor food in the conventional sense. But after the conversation with the dying demon, he felt more dead than the parched ground beneath his soles.

Ahead, through the dusty veil and leaden clouds, the broken skeletons of the ruined Observatory of Sivarus emerged. Once, in a former, bright life, this majestic building of white stone served as a place where mages studied the stars and the very fabric of the universe. Now, all that remained of it were rotten columns sticking out like the ribs of a dead whale and a half-collapsed dome.

The path to the ruins was blocked by a pack of scavengers. A dozen and a half lower scavenger demons—ugly, hunched creatures covered in festering sores and stiff, porcupine-like quills. They were tearing apart the decomposing, foul-smelling carcass of a dead winged serpent with loud, sickening smacking noises. Hearing the footsteps of a lone armored traveler, the creatures tore themselves away from the feast with a snarl. A predatory, carnivorous hunger flared in their faceted eyes.
They decided that in front of them was easy prey, tired from a long journey.
They did not realize that they had encountered death itself, looking only for an excuse to vent its corrosive pain.

Avotu didn't even slow his pace. His mind plunged into a familiar state of psychological dissociation—the protective barrier of PTSD, when feelings are switched off, leaving only a naked, centuries-honed combat instinct. He watched his own actions as if from the side. A hand in a plate gauntlet smoothly, without a single extra movement, rested on the worn hilt of a sword. As soon as the meteorite-forged blade hidden in the scabbard came into contact with the dead air of the wasteland, the Nature's Blade came to life.

Sivarus's special enchantment forced this ancient weapon to absorb the aggressive power of the environment. There were no lightning bolts or flames around—only parching death, icy wind, and flying ash.

The sword's blade began to turn black rapidly, covered by a monstrously dense, furiously rotating aura of abrasive dust. The sword emitted a low, vibrating hum, painfully similar to the whisper of a nascent tornado.
With a piercing scream, the scavengers rushed at the elf from all sides, haphazardly waving bone blades growing directly from their forearms.

Avotu made an elusive lunge. The first swing of the sword didn't even touch the demon's flesh—a compressed squall of stone and dust, torn from the blade under pressure, hit the first creature in the chest. In the blink of an eye, the magical sandpaper, with a sickening wet crunch, ground down the chitinous armor, muscles, and organs to the very bones. The demon's cry of pain drowned in the howling of the artificial wind.

The elf moved through the enemy ranks with a frightening, hypnotic grace. His living black flame on his head swayed slightly in time with the deadly dance, devoid of rage, full only of cold emptiness. The Nature's Blade ground the scavengers' bodies into bloody pulp, mixing their rotten blood with gray mud. Severed limbs fell into the dust, splashes of black ichor hissed on the elf's armor.

A minute and a half later, it was all over. The silence of the wastelands was broken only by the whistle of the wind. Near the ruins of the Observatory, nothing remained but remains hacked into mincemeat. Avotu smoothly swung his sword, shaking off the clotted bloody ash from the blade, and with a click drove the weapon into its scabbard. The artificial storm around immediately subsided.

Leaving the brutal carnage behind, he stepped under the splintered vault of the dome. Inside it was dark and damp, like a crypt. He had to step over decayed folios that had turned into dust and fragments of beautiful marble statues. He knew every stone of this place.
He was interested in the altar hall in the very center. Having made his way there, Avotu froze. His breath caught, and a rusty knife seemed to twist in his chest.

On the semi-intact western wall, hidden under a layer of dust, a huge mural was preserved. It was painted in those times when the sky was still clear. It depicted a laughing wood elf with eyes the color of spring needles. Biotrix.

Avotu slowly sank to his knees before the cold stone. The great warrior, who had just torn a dozen demons to pieces with his bare hands, removed his plate gauntlet and with trembling, disobedient fingers touched the painted face. The cold stone could not replace the warmth of her skin.

"I have no reason to fight..." Avotu said in a broken whisper into the emptiness of the ruined hall. The fragments of his broken psyche bled, deprived of the last support in this rotting world. "If there is no meaning in this chaos... then let me just disappear. Let me forget."
